hooks.command: input=$(cat); file_path=$(printf '%s' "$input" | jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // empty'); if [ -z "$file_path" ]; then exit 0; fi; case "$file_path" in *.py) if command -v black >/dev/null 2>&1; then black --quiet "$file_path"; fi; if command -v ruff >/dev/null 2>&1; then ruff check --fix --quiet "$file_path" || true; fi ;; esac

input=$(cat); fp=$(printf '%s' "$input" | jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // empty'); case "$fp" in */docs/progress-v1.md) printf '%s' '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PostToolUse","additionalContext":"docs/progress-v1.md is the FROZEN v1 archive (banner says do not update) - the live tracker is docs/progress.md. Make sure this edit is intentional archive maintenance, not a task tick that belongs in progress.md."}}';; */docs/progress.md) printf '%s' '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PostToolUse","additionalContext":"docs/progress.md changed - keep the Snapshot counts (open PRs/issues) consistent with GitHub, and if a tracked cycle epic exists (label:epic), tick/untick its checklist to match. The v1 epics #173-#177 closed with v1.0.0; the v1 ledger is the frozen docs/progress-v1.md."}}';; esac

input=$(cat); cmd=$(printf '%s' "$input" | jq -r '.tool_input.command // empty'); if printf '%s' "$cmd" | grep -Eq '(^|[[:space:];&|])[[:space:]]*gh pr merge([[:space:]]|$)'; then printf '%s' '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PostToolUse","additionalContext":"PR merged - per the PR-template checkbox, update docs/progress.md now: flip the cycle-plan / carried-over rows this PR implements and refresh the Snapshot counts (open PRs/issues); touch CLAUDE.md section 13 only if the headline changed. Tooling/docs PRs that ticked the N/A checkbox need no update."}}'; fi
sh -c 'if ! command -v entire >/dev/null 2>&1; then exit 0; fi; exec entire hooks claude-code post-task'
sh -c 'if ! command -v entire >/dev/null 2>&1; then exit 0; fi; exec entire hooks claude-code post-todo'

input=$(cat); file_path=$(printf '%s' "$input" | jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // empty'); if [ -z "$file_path" ]; then exit 0; fi; if printf '%s' "$file_path" | grep -qE '(^|/)\.env($|\.)' && ! printf '%s' "$file_path" | grep -qE '\.env(\.[^/]+)?\.example$'; then echo 'Blocked: .env files must not be created or edited (public repo). Use .env.example as the template.' >&2; exit 2; fi
sh -c 'if ! command -v entire >/dev/null 2>&1; then exit 0; fi; exec entire hooks claude-code pre-task'
sh -c 'if ! command -v entire >/dev/null 2>&1; then exit 0; fi; exec entire hooks claude-code session-end'

sh -c 'if ! command -v entire >/dev/null 2>&1; then printf "%s\n" "{\"systemMessage\":\"\\n\\nEntire CLI is enabled but not installed or not on PATH.\\nInstallation guide: https://docs.entire.io/cli/installation#installation-methods\"}"; exit 0; fi; exec entire hooks claude-code session-start'
sh -c 'if ! command -v entire >/dev/null 2>&1; then exit 0; fi; exec entire hooks claude-code stop'
sh -c 'if ! command -v entire >/dev/null 2>&1; then exit 0; fi; exec entire hooks claude-code user-prompt-submit'
